name: Gor and Charches Loop Trail
name_original: Valle del Zalabí – circular desde Gor por Charches – Parque Natural Sierra de Baza
description: This difficult 25.3-mile mountain biking route through Parque Natural Sierra de Baza offers challenging terrain and stunning views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Gor, Spain, is characterized by its diverse and rugged terrain, including the dramatic badlands of Desierto de Los Coloraos and the mountainous landscapes of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes navigating through natural parks and varied geological formations. This area offers a mix of challenging climbs and descents across its trails. Best mountain bike trails around Gor The most popular mountain bike trail is Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular from Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera, a 15.8 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the unique badlands terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valle del Zalabí – circular from Gor through Charches – Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, a difficult 25.3 miles (40.7 km) path. This route offers extensive views within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. Local mountain bikers also love the Prados del Rey a Puente de Baúl — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail leading through Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, often completed in about 5 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Gor is defined by the mountainous terrain of Sierra de Baza Natural Park and the distinct badlands. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Gor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 270 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking around Gor?
A: Mountain biking around Gor features diverse and rugged terrain, including the dramatic badlands of Desierto de Los Coloraos and the mountainous landscapes of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with routes navigating through natural parks and varied geological formations, offering a mix of challenging climbs and descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or easy rides in Gor?
A: While many trails in Gor are rated moderate to difficult, there are options that cater to different skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013299548/desierto-de-los-coloraos-circular-from-gorafe-badlands-castillejar-y-galera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular from Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 15.8-mile route that explores unique badlands terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: The region is known for its stunning natural beauty. You can expect extensive views within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, particularly on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155444106/valle-del-zalabi-circular-from-gor-through-charches-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Zalabí – circular from Gor through Charches – Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt;. The unique badlands terrain of the Desierto de Los Coloraos also offers distinctive landscapes. Additionally, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available in Gor?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Gor are circular. Popular options include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013299548/desierto-de-los-coloraos-circular-from-gorafe-badlands-castillejar-y-galera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular from Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013321308/prados-del-rey-a-puente-de-baul-circular-por-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prados del Rey a Puente de Baúl — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 37.5-mile loop through Parque Natural Sierra de Baza.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Gor?
A: The mountain bike routes in Gor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, from the unique badlands to the extensive views within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park.

Q: Are there any facilities or amenities along the mountain bike trails?
A: While the trails traverse natural areas, you can find some facilities nearby. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortijo Narváez Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is a useful stop. For accommodation or shelter, options like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canaleja Alta Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3403778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Cyclists' Shelter&lt;/a&gt; are available. If you need bike services, there's &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7340918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Cueva Bike Rental & Repair Shop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Gor typically take to complete?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on difficulty and length. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013299548/desierto-de-los-coloraos-circular-from-gorafe-badlands-castillejar-y-galera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular from Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera&lt;/a&gt; (15.8 miles) can take around 2 hours 30 minutes. More difficult and longer trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155444106/valle-del-zalabi-circular-from-gor-through-charches-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Zalabí – circular from Gor through Charches – Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt; (25.3 miles), might take closer to 4 hours, while the 37.5-mile &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013321308/prados-del-rey-a-puente-de-baul-circular-por-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prados del Rey a Puente de Baúl — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt; can take about 5 hours 30 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest I can visit near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, several interesting points are accessible. You can explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt;, visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3241591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baúl Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt;. For a different kind of path, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6070444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway gravel path: Via Verde, Baza - Baúl&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic route.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Gor?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, especially in the badlands areas, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially some snow at higher elevations within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails in Gor?
A: While specific crowd levels can vary, exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1132978819/los-prados-del-rey-desde-cortijo-narvaez-circular-por-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Prados del Rey desde Cortijo Narváez — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience compared to the most popular trails. Generally, weekdays and off-peak seasons tend to be less crowded.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Gor?
A: While the guide highlights moderate to difficult trails, families looking for gentler rides might consider shorter sections of the moderate routes or explore areas around the base of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013299548/desierto-de-los-coloraos-circular-from-gorafe-badlands-castillejar-y-galera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular from Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera&lt;/a&gt;, being moderate, could be an option for families with some biking experience.
tips: From this point the views are incredible! The mountains rise around the town of Las Juntas leaving a postcard image.
Nice path parallel to the ravine with a moderate drop of between 4 and 6%. The path allows you to enjoy fresh riverside vegetation.

This controlled camping area is located in the municipality of Charches. It has picnic areas, sinks and a fountain. The interior of the shelter is aimed at providing shelter in case of inclement weather; It has a couple of bathrooms and a fireplace. To camp, it is necessary to request an authorization from the Territorial Delegation of Sustainable Development of Granada.

Nestled in the Zalabí Valley, Charches is a town of uncertain origin, it is believed to have been founded in the 16th century. Despite the difficult living conditions and being considered marginalized, he has experienced significant improvements, especially in the cattle industry.
Charming path with slopes of between 6 and 13% where you can enjoy the views of the mountains that surround the path, where the Cerro del Perú stands out.
At this point on the road, the Sierra Nevada National Park can be seen in the distance. A charming landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 40.7 km long with an elevation gain of about 950 meters. It typically takes around 3 hours and 52 minutes to complete, but this can vary based on your fitness level and riding speed, especially given its difficult rating.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is considered difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It corresponds to STS S3 - S6, indicating technical terrain that requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. You might even need to push your bike in some sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied, reflecting the diverse landscape of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. You'll encounter both asphalted sections and challenging unpaved segments. The route follows the Gor river, winding through ancient cultivated terraces, rocky outcrops, and clay hills, with some areas showing remnants of old mining operations.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking views of the natural park, including a View of Sierra Nevada. The route passes through historic villages like Gor and Charches, and offers glimpses of traditional farmhouses. Highlights include the View of Las Juntas and the unique 'primitive landscape' of Valle del Zalabí with its cultivated meadows and pine groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Valle del Zalabí mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Gor. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking within or on the outskirts of Gor to begin your ride. As it's a circular route, you'll finish where you started.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for mountain biking in Parque Natural Sierra de Baza?
faqs.answer: As this route is within the Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, it's always a good idea to check for any specific regulations regarding mountain biking, especially concerning protected areas. While no specific permits are mentioned for this trail, respecting park rules, staying on marked paths, and protecting the environment are essential.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed in natural parks like Sierra de Baza, but they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the diverse fauna in the area. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Baza Natural Park experiences an extreme climate. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold, especially at higher altitudes, so check the seasonal weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant part of the trail passes through the 'La Fraguara' area. Here, you'll find a controlled camping site and a consistent water source, which can be very useful during your ride. This area has been developed as a recreational spot.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), the Path of the Barranco de las Casas de Don Diego, and various segments of the Cañada Real del Camino Real de Lorca, including the Cañada del Camino Real de Lorca.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Gor and Charches, which are part of the Valle del Zalabí municipality. These villages may offer local cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options. Additionally, the 'La Fraguara' area includes a controlled camping site if you're looking for an outdoor stay.
